# Maine Padel at the Downs — Complete Information

> Maine's first premier padel club, coming to The Downs in Scarborough, Maine. Join the Priority Access list at https://padelmaine.com

## What is Padel?

Padel is a racket sport played in a glass-walled enclosed court roughly one-third the size of a tennis court. It combines the best elements of tennis and squash into a fast-paced, highly social game that players of all ages and abilities can enjoy. Most beginners pick up the basics within their first hour of play.

The sport was invented in Acapulco, Mexico in 1969 by Enrique Corcuera. It was then adopted enthusiastically in Spain, where it became a national obsession. Today padel is played in over 78 countries with more than 25 million active players worldwide. It is the fastest-growing racket sport on Earth.

### Padel by the Numbers

- 8 million players in Europe
- 4 million players in Latin America
- 250,000 players in the United States (growing 40% year-over-year)
- 78+ countries with active padel communities
- $2.8 billion global padel market (2026)

## About Maine Padel at the Downs

Maine Padel at the Downs is a premier padel club planned for The Downs, a landmark mixed-use development in Scarborough, Maine. It will be the first dedicated padel facility in the state of Maine.

### Facility Details

- **Courts**: 4 indoor climate-controlled courts and 2 outdoor covered courts
- **Dining**: Full-service bar and restaurant
- **Retail**: Pro shop with padel equipment and apparel
- **Amenities**: Modern locker rooms, event space, spectator viewing areas
- **Location**: The Downs, Scarborough, Maine 04074

### Pricing

- **Court bookings**: 90-minute sessions at $60–$120 per court (peak/off-peak rates)
- **Membership tiers**:
  - Explorer: $89/month
  - Club: $149/month
  - Unlimited: $199/month

### Revenue Streams

The club generates revenue through six primary channels:
1. Court bookings (peak and off-peak 90-minute sessions)
2. Membership subscriptions (three tiers)
3. Pro shop sales (equipment and apparel)
4. Restaurant and bar
5. Lessons and coaching programs
6. Events and tournaments

## The Downs, Scarborough

The Downs is Scarborough, Maine's most exciting new mixed-use community development. It provides an ideal location for Maine Padel due to its growing residential population, commercial amenities, and community-focused design.

## Leadership

**William Graham — Club Director**
20 years of experience in luxury hospitality and sports marketing with international padel experience. William is passionate about bringing world-class padel to Maine.

## Contact Information

- **Website**: https://padelmaine.com
- **Email**: william@padelmaine.com
- **Location**: The Downs, Scarborough, Maine 04074

## How to Get Involved

### Priority Access List
Join the Priority Access list at https://padelmaine.com/#priority-access to be among the first to play when the club opens. Priority Access members receive:
- Early membership offers
- Exclusive event invitations
- Founding member benefits

### Investment Inquiries
For investment inquiries, visit https://padelmaine.com/#contact or email william@padelmaine.com

### Frequently Asked Questions

**What is padel?**
Padel is a racket sport played on an enclosed glass-walled court about one-third the size of a tennis court. It combines elements of tennis and squash, and is easy for beginners to pick up while offering depth for advanced players.

**Where will Maine Padel be located?**
Maine Padel will be located at The Downs in Scarborough, Maine 04074.

**How many courts will there be?**
The facility will have 4 indoor climate-controlled courts and 2 outdoor covered courts, for a total of 6 courts.

**How much does it cost to play?**
Court bookings are available in 90-minute sessions ranging from $60 to $120 per court depending on peak or off-peak timing. Monthly membership plans start at $89/month.

**Is padel easy to learn?**
Yes. Most people can learn the basics of padel within their first hour of play. The smaller court size and underhand serve make it accessible to players of all ages and fitness levels.

**How can I join the Priority Access list?**
Visit https://padelmaine.com/#priority-access and enter your name and email to join the list.

**How can I contact Maine Padel?**
Email william@padelmaine.com or visit https://padelmaine.com/#contact to submit an inquiry.
